Educational music program • youth-friendly • school-ready

Program overview

Live from Third Street STEAM Lab helps parents, students, schools, and community groups learn about the classes, services, and learning experiences offered by Mario Garcia and Deon Brown. The program combines music, creative technology, rhythm, audio engineering, improvisation, visual design, and collaborative learning in a way that feels hands-on, inspiring, and easy to understand.

This digital guide introduces the program, explains who Mario and Deon are, shows the different class pathways, and helps visitors find the right fit based on age, interests, goals, and preferred learning style.

Mario-led programsPiano, ear training, MIDI, sound design, apps, audio-reactive visuals, live video effects, and media production.
Deon Brown-led programsDrums, beat building, rhythm math, groove, pocket, drum setup, musical communication, and movement-based rhythm learning.
Joint programsImprovisation, live sound, signal flow, recording, 360 media, showcases, community storytelling, and full session production.
Creative technologySound-to-visual apps, visual-to-sound tools, customizable live visuals, and Bluetooth keyboard-powered performance systems.

Find the best fit for each learner

If a family or student is not sure which class, workshop, or learning path is the best match, the Program Finder Survey helps guide them to the right answer.

What the survey doesUsers choose a survey, enter the participant name, answer multi-select questions, and receive a detailed personalized program match.
What the results includeRecommended Mario-led, Deon Brown-led, and joint program tracks with detailed reasons, outcomes, and best-fit explanations.
Best for families & schoolsThe guide works for parents, students, teachers, youth groups, schools, and community organizations exploring class options.
Send results by emailAfter the survey, the results page prepares a detailed email to livefromthird@gmail.com with space for additional notes.

How this guide works

The homepage opens into this program overview, where families and students can learn what the STEAM Lab offers before moving into the Program Finder Survey. From there, users choose the most fitting survey type, enter the participant name, and answer multiple-answer questions. The final results page provides organized, detail-rich program matches and allows the results to be sent directly to the program email.

What students experience

The program teaches technical and creative skills through real performance environments, music-making, problem-solving, and hands-on exploration.

🎚️ Sound & signal flow

Students learn how instruments, microphones, mixer channels, speakers, recording devices, cables, and live sound decisions work together.

🥁 Rhythm, harmony & feel

Students explore timing, subdivisions, beat building, piano layout, chords, ear training, groove, improvisation, and musical communication.

✨ Apps, visuals & media

Students use custom apps that turn sound into visuals, visuals into sound, and live video into customizable effects while also learning recording and storytelling.

STEAM elements inside the program

Each class connects back to science, technology, engineering, arts, and math through real tools used in live creative production.

S

Science

Sound waves, vibration, frequency, resonance, acoustics, microphones, and outdoor listening environments.

T

Technology

MIDI, Logic, custom apps, Bluetooth keyboard control, audio-reactive visuals, live video effects, and 360 media.

E

Engineering

Signal flow, mixer routing, cable paths, speaker placement, drum miking, power planning, and troubleshooting.

A

Arts

Improvisation, feel, harmony, rhythm, composition, visual design, performance, and community storytelling.

M

Math

BPM, subdivisions, measures, looping, intervals, chord structure, song form, and rhythm patterns.

Who we are

Live from Third Street is led by Mario Garcia and Deon Brown, two experienced musicians using live performance as a doorway into STEAM learning, community service, and creative technology.

Mario Garcia

Mario leads the piano, MIDI, ear training, sound design, app-based learning, audio-reactive visual, visual-to-sound, live video effect, and media production sides of the program.

  • Piano, guitar, bass, MIDI, Logic on iPad, live sound, and creative technology
  • Custom apps for sound layers, textures, visual effects, ear training, and performance control
  • Bluetooth keyboard-controlled creative systems for live sound and visual design
  • 360 video capture, direct mixer audio recording, editing, presentation, and social media storytelling

Deon Brown

Deon leads the rhythm, drums, beat building, pocket, kit setup, cymbal selection, groove, and drummer communication sides of the program.

  • Drum set assembly, kit orientation, cymbal choices, tone, feel, and performance decisions
  • Beat building, looping, rhythm math, timing, subdivisions, dynamics, and groove
  • Drum language, musical communication, call-and-response, and supporting a live band
  • Real-world performance experience across varied live settings and environments

The Live from Third Street approach

Mario and Deon specialize in improvisational music creation. Their sessions often begin without a fixed destination, allowing listening, trust, rhythm, harmony, intuition, and technical skill to guide the music. The educational side of the program teaches students how that creative process works from the inside: how a setup is built, how a sound is shaped, how a rhythm leads, how visuals respond, how a recording is captured, and how a community moment becomes a finished story.

STEAM framework

Live from Third Street STEAM Lab connects the five parts of STEAM to the real tools and decisions behind a live session.

S

Science

Sound waves, vibration, frequency, resonance, acoustics, microphones, and outdoor listening environments.

T

Technology

MIDI, Logic, custom apps, Bluetooth keyboard control, audio-reactive visuals, live video effects, and 360 media.

E

Engineering

Signal flow, mixer routing, cable paths, speaker placement, drum miking, power planning, and troubleshooting.

A

Arts

Improvisation, feel, harmony, rhythm, composition, visual design, performance, and community storytelling.

M

Math

BPM, subdivisions, measures, looping, intervals, chord structure, song form, and rhythm patterns.
